subject: xpad: support for extra buttons on Flydigi Vader 3/4/5 Pro in wired and RF dongle modes.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Flydigi Vader 3/4/5 Pro extra buttons (C/Z/M1-M4/Circle/Turbo) will not be reported to userspace; controller falls back to basic XInput functionality.

userspace_coupling.interface: input event codes (EV_KEY events for BTN_C, BTN_Z, BTN_GRIPL, BTN_GRIPL2, BTN_GRIPR, BTN_GRIPR2, BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY1-4; abs events for analog sticks/triggers via Flydigi V2 protocol) reach userspace through Main_MiSTer's generic per-device button-mapping loop, not any Flydigi-specific code
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: input.cpp:3673-3680 (generic loop: `for (int i = 0; i < SYS_BTN_A; i++) if (ev->code == input[dev].mmap[i]) joy_digital(...)` — dispatches any EV_KEY code the mmap[] table recognizes, populated from def_mmap[] defaults / user .map files; no Flydigi-, Vader-, or 37d7-specific code exists in Main_MiSTer, verified by grep with no hits)

notes: Carried as delta 4 of patch 0017-xpad-mister-deltas.patch. Vanilla 6.18.38 has only Flydigi Apex 5 (0x37d7:0x2501) with no special button mapping and no Vader support. The commit is re-implemented (not clean-apply) in the carried patch: forward-port added NULL check on udev->product before strstr() to prevent crashes on devices without product descriptor, changed kmalloc failure return from -1 to -ENOMEM, and nulled flydigi_idata after URB allocation failure. Flydigi V2 protocol toggles extended mode via Circle+Turbo chord, exposing C/Z/M1-M4/LM/RM and Turbo button events. Hardware verification pending per patch-provenance.md line 1148. CORRECTION (sonnet review): main_mister_ref was previously null despite userspace_coupling.coupled=true — this is exactly the escalation trigger worker-instructions.md warns about ('you assert userspace coupling without a Main_MiSTer path:line citation'). Filled in with the generic mechanism that actually carries these events: input.cpp:3673-3680 compares every incoming EV_KEY code against the per-device mmap[] table and forwards matches to joy_digital(), with no Flydigi-specific code anywhere in Main_MiSTer (grep for Flydigi/Vader/37d7 across Main_MiSTer/*.cpp and *.h returns no hits).
sonnet-verified: carry-mapping into 0017 confirmed (0x37d7:0x2401 Vader 5 Pro entry, MAP_VADER4/MAP_VADER5 flags, VADER3/VADER4 product-string detection all match the fork commit); AMENDED a grounding-contract violation: userspace_coupling.coupled=true had main_mister_ref=null (an explicit escalation trigger) — added input.cpp:3673-3680 as the actual (generic, non-Flydigi-specific) citation
